title: Pan-Roasted Greek Lamb Souvlaki Kadhai
description: A crispy and herbal twist on traditional Greek lamb souvlaki, cooked to perfection in a shallow kadhai with a hint of chilled butter and deglazed with red wine.

ingredients.name: boneless lamb shoulder or leg, cut into 1-inch souvlaki pieces
ingredients.notes: preferably from a Greek or Mediterranean market

ingredients.name: red wine
ingredients.notes: preferably a dry, full-bodied wine like Cabernet Sauvignon or Syrah

ingredients.name: Greek yogurt (optional)
ingredients.notes: serve on the side, if desired

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, oregano, thyme, salt, and pepper to make a marinade.
Add the lamb souvlaki pieces to the marinade and toss to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat a shallow kadhai or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the lamb souvlaki pieces and sear, without moving, until browned on the bottom, about 3-4 minutes.
Flip the lamb and sear the other side, another 3-4 minutes.
Transfer the kadhai to the preheated oven and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the kadhai from the oven and brush the lamb with the chilled butter. Return the kadhai to the stovetop over medium heat.
Deglaze the kadhai with the red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon.
Simmer the sauce for 2-3 minutes, until slightly thickened and syrupy.
Serve the lamb souvlaki hot, garnished with fresh oregano and a dollop of Greek yogurt, if desired.
